🎙️ NMVU Financial Model Walkthrough – Voiceover Transcript (Final Version)

[Opening – 0:00–0:30] “Welcome to your NMVU Financial Projections Model. This model was designed to help you understand how your business could perform over the next three years — even if you’re new to finance or Excel. You’ll enter a few simple assumptions, and the model will automatically calculate your profit, cash flow, and key metrics to show you what’s possible for your business.”



[Assumptions Tab – 0:30–2:00] “Let’s start with the first tab: Assumptions. This is the only place you’ll be typing. Everything else in the model will update automatically based on what you enter here.

You’ll begin by entering your revenue assumptions — things like your average sale price, number of customers, or how many units you expect to sell each month. Next, you’ll move to your cost assumptions. These might include materials, packaging, or other direct costs that go into producing your product or service. Then, fill i

[Opening – 0:00–0:30] “Welcome to your NMVU Financial Projections Model. This model was designed to help you understand how your business could perform over the next three years — even if you’re new to finance or Excel. You’ll enter a few simple assumptions, and the model will automatically calculate your profit, cash flow, and key metrics to show you what’s possible for your business.”



[Assumptions Tab – 0:30–2:00] “Let’s start with the first tab: Assumptions. This is the only place you’ll be typing. Everything else in the model will update automatically based on what you enter here.

You’ll begin by entering your revenue assumptions — things like your average sale price, number of customers, or how many units you expect to sell each month. ette visually narrates the entrepreneurial journey
beginning with Catalyst Chartreuse (F2E240), transitioning through
Growth Green (3A913A) and Strategic Emerald (23573C) for decisive
action, deepening into Action Teal (183A3D) and Focus Navy
(0C1C3D) as the path forward, with Innovation Blue (7D90FD) and
Success Sky (B1BAFC) marking breakthrough achievements, and
anchored by Heritage Earth (522B29) honoring the rich soil of future
growth and the diverse heritage of the global majority.

The Main Logo
Our signature NMV logo is reimagined through these strategic
colorways. By applying our refreshed palette to the established
mark, we maintain brand recognition while visually aligning with
our mission to support Global Majority entrepreneurs. Each
variation reflects a different facet of o

ity financing is not well aligned with your current stage or operating reality. Pursuing it now could introduce unnecessary dilution, misaligned expectations, or execution risk.

Recommended focus areas Focus on building a stronger operating foundation. Improve revenue predictability and unit economics. Delay fundraising until the business is better positioned for equity capital.



Equity financing may introduce misalignment, pressure, or loss of control at this stage. Next step: Consider bootstrapping, revenue-based growth, debt, or grants.



Optional NMVU Badge Logic

80+ points required to unlock:

Equity Capital credential

Advanced Capital Strategy content

NMVU Grant Funding Resource
This resource helps founders evaluate, prepare for, and pursue grant funding intentionally. Use it
alongside the Capital Readiness Assessment and Grant Funding lesson.
1. What Grants Are (and Are Not)
Grants are non-dilutive, non-repayable funds awarded for specific missions or outcomes. They are not
fast money or flexible capital. Grants reward alignment, clarity, and execution.
2. Grant Readiness Checklist
•
Registered business entity with EIN
•
Business bank account and basic bookkeeping system
•
Clear mission and impact statement
•
Defined project or use of funds
•
Ability to track and report outcomes
•
Time and capacity to manage applications and reporting
3. Common Grant Categories
Grant TypeTypical FocusBest For
GovernmentResearch, innovation, economic developmentStructured businesses
FoundationCommunity impact, entrepreneurshipMission-driven founders
CorporateInnovation, supplier diversityEarly traction businesses
Industry-SpecificSector or demograph

NMVU Venture Debt Decision Worksheet
This worksheet helps you pressure-test whether venture debt is an appropriate financing tool for your
business. Use it alongside the Venture Debt Readiness Assessment to evaluate risk, timing, and
financial impact before pursuing lenders.
1. Company Snapshot
Current Revenue (ARR): ____________________
Monthly Burn Rate: ____________________
Current Runway (months): ____________________
Most Recent Equity Round ($ / date): ____________________
2. Proposed Venture Debt Terms
Loan AmountInterest RateTerm LengthWarrantsCovenants
3. Use of Proceeds
Describe exactly how the debt capital will be used and how it is expected to extend runway or generate
return.
4. Runway Impact Analysis
How many months of runway does this debt add? What happens if revenue growth slows for 3–6
months?
5. Risk Check
List the top three risks introduced by taking on venture debt (cash pressure, covenants, flexibility, etc.).
6. Decision Summary
Based on this analysis, is venture

downside strategy

Would taking on this debt limit future equity fundraising?

Yes

Possibly

No

Unsure

Do you understand the covenants and default triggers in venture debt agreements?

No

Somewhat

Yes



SCORING OUTPUT (NMVU-style)

Not Venture Debt Ready

Foundational financial or cash flow risk present

Conditionally Ready

Eligible with specific fixes (forecasting, margins, concentration)

Venture Debt Ready

Predictable revenue, strong margins, downside protected
